bids.reasoning: This is the opening bid at t=0, so I should start with my best possible offer - all valueC across all issues, which gives me maximum utility of 1.0. There's no offer history to read from, so I'll anchor high and concede later as needed. All issues have valueC as top utility, so this is straightforwardly my ideal bid.

The opponent offered all valueA across every issue, which gives me minimum utility (0.2 for each issue), while claiming it's "win-win" — this is clearly self-serving. It's very early (t=0.02), so I should stay near my maximum utility bid. I'll repeat essentially my top bid (all valueC) since this is still the opening phase and there's no reason to concede yet. The opponent's message is cheap talk contradicted by their numbers.

We're very early (t=0.04) and the opponent is stubbornly bidding all-valueA (their best, my worst) while claiming it's "win-win" — a clear contradiction. Their messages are cheap talk. My utility for all-valueA is 0.20 across all issues = 0.20 total, which is terrible. I should hold firm near my maximum utility bid (all-valueC = 1.0) at this stage. I'll repeat my top bid since there's no reason to concede yet.

The opponent is consistently offering all-valueA (utility 0.20 for me on everything) while claiming it's "win-win" - this is clearly a lopsided offer in their favor. We're only at t=0.06, very early in the negotiation. I should hold firm near my maximum utility (all valueC) since there's no reason to concede yet. The opponent hasn't moved at all from their initial position, so I'll maintain my strong position.

The opponent is consistently bidding all-valueA (utility 0.2 for me) while claiming it's "win-win" — a clear mismatch between words and actions. We're only at t=0.08, very early in the negotiation. I should hold firm at my maximum utility bid (all-valueC) for now. There's no reason to concede yet given the opponent hasn't moved at all from their opening position.

The opponent has been consistently offering all valueA (their best, my worst) except for a small concession on issueD to valueB in their latest offer. My utility from their latest offer is: 0.1*0.2 + 0.1*0.2 + 0.2*0.2 + 0.2*0.61 + 0.2*0.2 + 0.3*0.2 = 0.02+0.02+0.04+0.122+0.04+0.06 = 0.302. We're only at t=0.10, so there's no need to concede much. I'll make a small concession on the lowest-weight issues (issueA and issueB) by moving to valueB, while holding firm on the more important issues. My utility: 0.1*0.61 + 0.1*0.61 + 0.2*1.0 + 0.2*1.0 + 0.2*1.0 + 0.3*1.0 = 0.061+0.061+0.2+0.2+0.2+0.3 = 1.022 → actually 0.922. This shows some movement without sacrificing much.

The opponent is barely conceding - they've only moved issueD from valueA to valueB across many rounds, while demanding valueA on everything else (which gives me minimum utility of 0.20 on each issue). Their messages claim fairness but their offers are extremely lopsided in their favor. At t=0.12, we're still very early - I should stay firm near my optimum. I'll make a slight concession on the low-weight issues (issueA, issueB) to signal some flexibility while keeping my high-weight issues (issueF, issueE, issueC, issueD) at valueC.

The opponent has been stubbornly holding at all-valueA offers except for issueD (moved to valueB). Their messages claim fairness but the bids are clearly maximizing their own utility (all valueA = 0.2 utility for me). We're only at t=0.14, so there's no pressure to concede heavily yet. My last offer gave valueB on issueA and issueB (low-weight issues) while keeping valueC on the high-weight issues (C, D, E, F). I should maintain a strong position but slightly vary to show movement while protecting my high-weight issues. I'll keep my previous bid structure but perhaps nudge issueD slightly since the opponent seems to value it (they conceded there), keeping it at valueC since it matters equally to me.

The opponent has been consistently offering all valueA, with minor concessions (issueD moved to valueB in t=0.10-0.14, then back to valueA while moving issueF to valueB at t=0.16). Their offers give me very low utility (around 0.20 each issue = ~0.20 total utility). My offers give me maximum utility (1.0). We're still early (t=0.16), so I don't need to make major concessions yet. However, I should start making small strategic concessions on low-weight issues (issueA, issueB weight=0.1) to signal willingness while holding firm on high-weight issues (issueF weight=0.3, issueC/D/E weight=0.2). The opponent moved on issueF to valueB - I should respond by conceding on my low-weight issues to keep momentum while protecting my high-weight issues.
